title: dimocarpus fumatus subsp. indochinensis
intro: Dimocarpus fumatus subsp. indochinensis, also known as the velvet apple, is a species of plant in the Sapindaceae family, native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Dimocarpus fumatus subsp. indochinensis include reducing inflammation, treating fever and diarrhea, and protecting against oxidative stress and cell damage. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves, bark, and roots are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures that can be used to treat various health conditions.
